steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: The terms are $5x^5$, $-6x^4$, and $8x^3$. The coefficients $5, 6, 8$ share no common integer factor greater than $1$, and the lowest power of $x$ is $x^3$. So the GCF is $x^3$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $x^4(5x - 6)$ expands to $5x^5 - 6x^4$, missing the $8x^3$ term. Eliminate.
  • B: $x^3(5x^2 - 6x + 8)$ expands to $5x^5 - 6x^4 + 8x^3$. Keep.
  • C: $8x^3(5x^2 - 6x + 1)$ expands to $40x^5 - 48x^4 + 8x^3$; wrong coefficients. Eliminate.
  • D: $6x^5(-6x^4 + 8x^3 + 1)$ produces an $x^9$ term, which does not appear in the original. Eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
